1	-- schema.sql — applied by sqlite.Open on every call (idempotent via
2	-- CREATE ... IF NOT EXISTS). Kept as a single file per the pitch's
3	-- no-migration-library stance: if the schema ever changes before v1.0,
4	-- users re-run `sense scan` and the index rebuilds from source.
5	
6	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sense_files (
7	    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
8	    path        TEXT    NOT NULL UNIQUE,
9	    language    TEXT    NOT NULL,
10	    hash        TEXT    NOT NULL,
11	    symbols     INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
12	    indexed_at  TEXT    NOT NULL
13	);
14	
15	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sense_files_path     ON sense_files (path);
16	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sense_files_language ON sense_files (language);
17	
18	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sense_symbols (
19	    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
20	    file_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES sense_files(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
21	    name        TEXT    NOT NULL,
22	    qualified   TEXT    NOT NULL,
23	    kind        TEXT    NOT NULL,
24	    visibility  TEXT    DEFAULT 'public',
25	    parent_id   INTEGER REFERENCES sense_symbols(id),
26	    line_start  INTEGER NOT NULL,
27	    line_end    INTEGER NOT NULL,
28	    docstring   TEXT,
29	    complexity  INTEGER,
30	    snippet     TEXT,
31	    name_parts  TEXT,
32	    -- (file_id, qualified) is the Index contract's natural key for Symbol
33	    -- upserts. Not in the definition doc but required for ON CONFLICT.
34	    UNIQUE (file_id, qualified)
35	);
36	
37	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sense_symbols_name      ON sense_symbols (name);
38	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sense_symbols_qualified ON sense_symbols (qualified);
39	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sense_symbols_kind      ON sense_symbols (kind);
40	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sense_symbols_file_id   ON sense_symbols (file_id);
41	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sense_symbols_parent_id ON sense_symbols (parent_id);
42	
43	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sense_edges (
44	    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
45	    source_id   INTEGER REFERENCES sense_symbols(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
46	    target_id   INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES sense_symbols(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
47	    kind        TEXT    NOT NULL,
48	    file_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES sense_files(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
49	    line        INTEGER,
50	    confidence  REAL    DEFAULT 1.0
51	);
52	
53	CREATE INDEX        IF NOT EXISTS idx_sense_edges_source ON sense_edges (source_id, kind);
54	
55	-- idx_sense_edges_target is the reverse-edge index the blast BFS
56	-- reads against: "given these target_ids, find source_ids for
57	-- kind='calls'." Including source_id makes the index COVERING for
58	-- that exact query shape so BFS walks with zero row fetches per
59	-- hop — `EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN` reports `USING COVERING INDEX` and
60	-- `internal/sqlite/plan_test.go` pins that guarantee. The DROP
61	-- below is an in-place upgrade path for databases that were created
62	-- before the index gained its third column; on a fresh DB it is a
63	-- no-op.
64	DROP   INDEX        IF EXISTS     idx_sense_edges_target;
65	CREATE INDEX        IF NOT EXISTS idx_sense_edges_target ON sense_edges (target_id, kind, source_id);
66	
67	CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sense_edges_unique ON sense_edges (source_id, target_id, kind, file_id);
68	
69	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sense_metrics (
70	    key   TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
71	    value INTEGER NOT NULL
72	);
73	
74	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sense_meta (
75	    key   TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
76	    value TEXT NOT NULL
77	);
78	
79	-- sense_embeddings is created empty now. Cycle 2's embedding pitch will
80	-- populate it; having the table upfront means that pitch needs no schema
81	-- change, just inserts.
82	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sense_embeddings (
83	    symbol_id   INTEGER PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES sense_symbols(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
84	    vector      BLOB    NOT NULL
85	);

1	// Package sqlite is the SQLite-backed implementation of index.Index. It
2	// uses modernc.org/sqlite (pure Go, CGO_ENABLED=0) so the single-binary
3	// story in 02-architecture.md holds.
4	package sqlite
5	
6	import (
7		"context"
8		"database/sql"
9		_ "embed"
10		"errors"
11		"fmt"
12		"strconv"
13		"strings"
14		"time"
15	
16		_ "modernc.org/sqlite" // registers the "sqlite" driver
17	
18		"github.com/luuuc/sense/internal/index"
19		"github.com/luuuc/sense/internal/model"
20	)
21	
22	//go:embed schema.sql
23	var schemaSQL string
24	
25	//go:embed schema_fts.sql
26	var schemaFTSSQL string
27	
28	// SchemaVersion is stamped into the database's PRAGMA user_version.
29	// Bump when schema.sql changes incompatibly — a mismatch triggers
30	// auto-rebuild (drop all tables, fresh schema, full scan). Never set
31	// before a scan completes successfully; see StampSchemaVersion.
32	const SchemaVersion = 4
33	
34	// maxOpenConns is the connection-pool size Open applies. InTx relies on
35	// this being 1 — its raw BEGIN/COMMIT approach shares a transaction
36	// across Adapter calls by sharing the single pooled connection. If this
37	// ever changes to allow concurrent writes, InTx must be reworked to use
38	// database/sql's proper *sql.Tx threading. The constant is checked at
39	// InTx entry so a future config change fails loudly instead of silently
40	// corrupting transactional semantics.
41	const maxOpenConns = 1
42	
43	// ftsTriggerStatements keep the FTS5 content-sync table in sync with
44	// sense_symbols. Each trigger is executed individually because
45	// modernc.org/sqlite's multi-statement handling silently drops trigger
46	// DDL after virtual-table statements.
47	var ftsTriggerStatements = []string{
48		`CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS sense_symbols_fts_insert
49		 AFTER INSERT ON sense_symbols BEGIN
50		     INSERT INTO sense_symbols_fts(rowid, name, qualified, docstring, snippet, name_parts)
51		     VALUES (new.id, new.name, new.qualified, new.docstring, new.snippet, new.name_parts);
52		 END`,
53	
54		`CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS sense_symbols_fts_delete
55		 BEFORE DELETE ON sense_symbols BEGIN
56		     INSERT INTO sense_symbols_fts(sense_symbols_fts, rowid, name, qualified, docstring, snippet, name_parts)
57		     VALUES ('delete', old.id, old.name, old.qualified, old.docstring, old.snippet, old.name_parts);
58		 END`,
59	
60		`CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS sense_symbols_fts_update
61		 BEFORE UPDATE ON sense_symbols BEGIN
62		     INSERT INTO sense_symbols_fts(sense_symbols_fts, rowid, name, qualified, docstring, snippet, name_parts)
63		     VALUES ('delete', old.id, old.name, old.qualified, old.docstring, old.snippet, old.name_parts);
64		 END`,
65	
66		`CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS sense_symbols_fts_update_after
67		 AFTER UPDATE ON sense_symbols BEGIN
68		     INSERT INTO sense_symbols_fts(rowid, name, qualified, docstring, snippet, name_parts)
69		     VALUES (new.id, new.name, new.qualified, new.docstring, new.snippet, new.name_parts);
70		 END`,
71	}
72	
73	// Adapter is the SQLite implementation of index.Index.
74	type Adapter struct {
75		db *sql.DB
76		// Rebuilt is true when Open detected a schema version mismatch and
77		// dropped all tables to recreate a fresh schema. Callers that need
78		// a populated index (e.g. the MCP server) should trigger a full
79		// scan before serving.
80		Rebuilt bool
81		// FTSMigrated is true when Open detected a stale FTS5 table
82		// (missing columns) and dropped+recreated it. Keyword search
83		// results will be incomplete until the next scan repopulates them.
84		FTSMigrated bool
85	}
86	
87	// compile-time contract check.
88	var _ index.Index = (*Adapter)(nil)
89	
90	// Open opens or creates a SQLite index at path. The parent directory must
91	// already exist. On every open the schema is reapplied (idempotent) and
92	// WAL + foreign-key pragmas are set via the DSN so they apply to every
93	// connection in the pool, not just the first.
94	func Open(ctx context.Context, path string) (*Adapter, error) {
95		// modernc.org/sqlite reads _pragma parameters on every new connection,
96		// which is what foreign_keys (a per-connection setting) requires.
97		// synchronous=NORMAL is durable under WAL for crash recovery; it can
98		// lose the last few seconds under a power cut. That's the right
99		// trade-off for a derived index — `sense scan` rebuilds from source.
100		mmapSize := "0"
101		if strconv.IntSize == 64 {
102			mmapSize = "134217728" // 128MB on 64-bit platforms
103		}
104		dsn := "file:" + path + "?_pragma=foreign_keys(1)" +
105			"&_pragma=journal_mode(wal)" +
106			"&_pragma=synchronous(normal)" +
107			"&_pragma=temp_store(memory)" +
108			"&_pragma=cache_size(-8000)" +
109			"&_pragma=mmap_size(" + mmapSize + ")"
110	
111		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dsn)
112		if err != nil {
113			return nil, fmt.Errorf("sqlite open: %w", err)
114		}
115	
116		// Single connection serializes writes and reads. Scan is sequential
117		// today; relax when concurrent MCP reads warrant the contention cost.
118		// Load-bearing for InTx — see maxOpenConns constant.
119		db.SetMaxOpenConns(maxOpenConns)
120	
121		// Check stored schema version. A mismatch (stored > 0 but !=
122		// expected) means the binary was upgraded — drop everything and
123		// rebuild from source. Version 0 means fresh DB (no prior scan).
124		// If PRAGMA user_version itself fails (corrupt file), storedVersion
125		// stays 0 and we proceed as fresh — the schema apply below will
126		// likely fail with a more descriptive error.
127		var storedVersion int
128		_ = db.QueryRowContext(ctx, "PRAGMA user_version").Scan(&storedVersion)
129		rebuilt := false
130		if storedVersion != 0 && storedVersion != SchemaVersion {
131			rebuilt = true
132			if err := dropAllSenseTables(ctx, db); err != nil {
133				_ = db.Close()
134				return nil, err
135			}
136			// Reset user_version so the state is indistinguishable from a
137			// fresh DB until StampSchemaVersion is called after scan.
138			if _, err := db.ExecContext(ctx, "PRAGMA user_version = 0"); err != nil {
139				_ = db.Close()
140				return nil, fmt.Errorf("sqlite reset user_version: %w", err)
141			}
142		}
143	
144		if _, err := db.ExecContext(ctx, schemaSQL); err != nil {
145			_ = db.Close()
146			return nil, fmt.Errorf("sqlite schema: %w", err)
147		}
148	
149		// FTS5 virtual table and triggers are applied separately because
150		// modernc.org/sqlite's ExecContext silently drops virtual-table
151		// statements embedded in a long multi-statement string.
152		//
153		// Migration: if the FTS table exists but is missing the snippet
154		// column (added in search-quality pitch), drop it and its triggers
155		// so the CREATE IF NOT EXISTS below picks up the new schema.
156		ftsMigrated := ftsNeedsMigration(ctx, db)
157		if ftsMigrated {
158			for _, name := range []string{
159				"sense_symbols_fts_insert", "sense_symbols_fts_delete",
160				"sense_symbols_fts_update", "sense_symbols_fts_update_after",
161			} {
162				_, _ = db.ExecContext(ctx, "DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS "+name)
163			}
164			_, _ = db.ExecContext(ctx, "DROP TABLE IF EXISTS sense_symbols_fts")
165		}
166	
167		if _, err := db.ExecContext(ctx, schemaFTSSQL); err != nil {
168			_ = db.Close()
169			return nil, fmt.Errorf("sqlite fts schema: %w", err)
170		}
171		for _, stmt := range ftsTriggerStatements {
172			if _, err := db.ExecContext(ctx, stmt); err != nil {
173				_ = db.Close()
174				return nil, fmt.Errorf("sqlite fts trigger: %w", err)
175			}
176		}
177	
178		return &Adapter{db: db, Rebuilt: rebuilt, FTSMigrated: ftsMigrated}, nil
179	}
180	
181	// ftsNeedsMigration returns true if the FTS5 table exists but is
182	// missing the snippet column. This happens when upgrading from a
183	// database created before the search-quality pitch added snippet
184	// to the FTS index.
185	func ftsNeedsMigration(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB) bool {
186		var count int
187		err := db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
188			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sqlite_master
189			 WHERE type='table' AND name='sense_symbols_fts'`).Scan(&count)
190		if err != nil || count == 0 {
191			return false
192		}
193		// FTS5 content tables expose column names via a zero-row SELECT.
194		rows, err := db.QueryContext(ctx, "SELECT * FROM sense_symbols_fts LIMIT 0")
195		if err != nil {
196			return false
197		}
198		defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
199		cols, err := rows.Columns()
200		if err != nil {
201			return false
202		}
203		has := map[string]bool{}
204		for _, c := range cols {
205			has[c] = true
206		}
207		return !has["snippet"] || !has["name_parts"]
208	}
209	
210	// StampSchemaVersion writes the current SchemaVersion into PRAGMA
211	// user_version. Call ONLY after a successful scan completes — this is
212	// the invariant that prevents a crash mid-rebuild from leaving an
213	// apparently-current but incomplete index.
214	func (a *Adapter) StampSchemaVersion(ctx context.Context) error {
215		_, err := a.db.ExecContext(ctx, fmt.Sprintf("PRAGMA user_version = %d", SchemaVersion))
216		if err != nil {
217			return fmt.Errorf("sqlite stamp schema version: %w", err)
218		}
219		return nil
220	}
221	
222	// Close releases the underlying database handle and flushes the WAL.
223	func (a *Adapter) Close() error {
224		return a.db.Close()
225	}
226	
227	// DB exposes the underlying *sql.DB for read-path consumers that
228	// speak plain database/sql — the blast BFS in 01-03 and the CLI
229	// symbol lookup in 01-04. Writers must continue to go through the
230	// named WriteFile / WriteSymbol / WriteEdge methods so the upsert
231	// contract stays in one place.
232	func (a *Adapter) DB() *sql.DB {
233		return a.db
234	}
235	
236	// InTx runs fn inside a SQLite transaction, committing on success and
237	// rolling back on error. Callers keep using the same Adapter inside fn —
238	// there is no transaction-scoped handle to thread through, which avoids
239	// an interface-wide refactor.
240	//
241	// This relies on MaxOpenConns being 1: with a single pooled connection,
242	// the BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK statements issued here share the same
243	// connection as every a.db.ExecContext / QueryRowContext call inside
244	// fn, so SQLite treats them as one transaction. If the pool size ever
245	// changes, this helper must be reworked to use database/sql's proper
246	// BeginTx + *sql.Tx plumbing. The runtime check below fails loudly
247	// rather than silently corrupting transactional semantics.
248	func (a *Adapter) InTx(ctx context.Context, fn func() error) (err error) {
249		if got := a.db.Stats().MaxOpenConnections; got != maxOpenConns {
250			panic(fmt.Sprintf(
251				"sqlite.InTx: MaxOpenConnections = %d, want %d — the single-conn "+
252					"transaction trick no longer applies; switch InTx to *sql.Tx "+
253					"plumbing before raising the pool size",
254				got, maxOpenConns))
255		}
256		if _, err := a.db.ExecContext(ctx, "BEGIN IMMEDIATE"); err != nil {
257			return fmt.Errorf("sqlite begin: %w", err)
258		}
259		defer func() {
260			if err != nil {
261				// Best-effort rollback; the returned error is the real one.
262				// A rollback failure after a primary error is almost always
263				// a consequence of the primary error, not new information.
264				_, _ = a.db.ExecContext(ctx, "ROLLBACK")
265				return
266			}
267			if _, commitErr := a.db.ExecContext(ctx, "COMMIT"); commitErr != nil {
268				err = fmt.Errorf("sqlite commit: %w", commitErr)
269				_, _ = a.db.ExecContext(ctx, "ROLLBACK")
270			}
271		}()
272		return fn()
273	}
